gfr.name: GFR Calculator
gfr.description: Calculate kidney function: eGFR using CKD-EPI 2021, CKD stage, and risk assessment.

meta.title: GFR Calculator — eGFR & Kidney Function | Health Calculators
meta.description: Calculate eGFR using CKD-EPI 2021 (race-free, KDIGO-recommended). Get your CKD stage G1–G5, risk assessment, and when to see a doctor. Free kidney function calculator.

gfr.title: GFR Calculator — eGFR Kidney Function
gfr.description: Calculate estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) using the CKD-EPI 2021 equation and assess kidney function.
gfr.formulaNote: Calculated using CKD-EPI 2021 (race-free, KDIGO-recommended). Not a substitute for medical diagnosis.
gfr.contextEquationText: The CKD-EPI 2021 equation is the current KDIGO (Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes) recommended formula. It is race-free and provides accurate estimates across all age and creatinine ranges.
gfr.contextDrugText: Many medications are dosed based on eGFR or are contraindicated with reduced kidney function, including metformin, NSAIDs, certain antibiotics, and contrast agents.
gfr.contextLimitsText: eGFR equations estimate kidney function — they do not directly measure it. Results may be less accurate in extreme muscle mass, malnutrition, acute kidney injury, or pregnancy.
gfr.contextDisclaimerText: This tool is for informational purposes only and does not replace medical evaluation. Abnormal kidney values should always be discussed with a healthcare provider.

risk.low: Normal to mildly elevated range. Regular check-ups recommended.
risk.moderate: Mildly to moderately elevated risk. Treat risk factors such as hypertension and diabetes.
risk.high: Elevated risk for kidney complications. Nephrology follow-up recommended.
risk.veryHigh: Very high risk. Specialized nephrology care required.
risk.kidney_failure: Kidney failure. Dialysis or transplantation may be necessary.

doctor.G2: Inform your primary care doctor and have risk factors (blood pressure, blood sugar) checked.
doctor.G3a: Regular follow-up with your primary care doctor or nephrologist recommended (at least annually).
doctor.G3b: See a nephrologist. Close monitoring and medication adjustments are needed.
doctor.G4: Urgent nephrology referral. Discuss preparation for kidney replacement therapy (dialysis/transplant).
doctor.G5: Immediate nephrology care required. Kidney replacement therapy is typically necessary.
